extraction.one_line_summary: ACKERMANN Pascal won Stage 5 bunch sprint in Geneva after TOPLEY attempted a surprise late solo attack that failed to create panic in the controlled finale.
tactical_patterns: TOPLEY attempted solo attack mimicking Nizzolo's historic Geneva sprint win, but Bora kept calm and reeled him efficiently.
Education First tried pre-finale attacks lacking sprinter, revealing teams without fast finishers probe flat finales for opportunistic wins.
Breakaway collapsed at 35 seconds when peloton formed single-file echelon, showing precise calibration by sprint trains.
BORA led out with all wheels covered, demonstrating dominant sprint train control after Ackermann's multiple near-misses this season.

rider_signals.ACKERMANN Pascal: positive: Finally converted after multiple second and third places this season, showing persistence in development pays off at 24.
rider_signals.TOPLEY Michael: neutral: Aggressive late attack showed tactical awareness referencing historical Geneva sprint upsets but lacked execution power.
rider_signals.PRIMOZ Roglic: positive: Won Tour de Romandie by 8 seconds over Egan Bernal, controlling final stage without incident.
rider_signals.PORTE Richie: neutral: Assured podium at 35 seconds, no attempt to challenge for higher position on final stage.
